articles.title: Lakers Take 2-0 Series Lead Over Rockets Despite Kevin Durant’s Return
articles.original_title: 3 Takeaways From Lakers Going Up 2-0 Despite Kevin Durant Returning for Houston - Sports Illustrated
articles.summary: The Los Angeles Lakers defeated the Houston Rockets 101-94 in Game 2, moving up 2-0 in the first‑round series. Even with Kevin Durant back in the lineup for Houston, the Lakers maintained control, highlighted by strong team defense and balanced scoring.
key_points: Lakers win 101-94, securing a 2-0 series advantage.
Kevin Durant returned for the Rockets but could not shift momentum.
Los Angeles relied on collective effort and defensive stops to limit Houston’s offense.

articles.title: LeBron Scores 28 as Short‑Handed Lakers Beat Rockets 101-94 in Game 2
articles.original_title: LeBron scores 28, leads short-handed Lakers past Durant's Rockets again 101-94 for 2-0 series lead - Stamford Advocate
articles.summary: LeBron James led a depleted Lakers roster with 28 points, guiding the team to a 101-94 victory over the Houston Rockets and extending the series lead to 2-0. The win came despite Houston’s boost from Kevin Durant’s return.
key_points: LeBron James contributed 28 points in a short‑handed lineup.
Lakers held the Rockets to 94 points, winning by seven.
The victory puts the Lakers two games away from advancing.

articles.title: Lakers Grind Out Game 2 Win Over Rockets

articles.summary: The Los Angeles Lakers defeated the Houston Rockets 101‑94 in Game 2, taking a 2‑0 series lead. Scoring was scarce throughout the contest, with both teams relying heavily on defense after a higher‑scoring Game 1.
key_points: Lakers improved to 2‑0 in the first‑round series.
Final score was 101‑94, reflecting a defensive battle.
Offensive production was limited for both clubs compared with Game 1.

articles.title: Lakers Beat Rockets to Take 2‑0 Series Lead

articles.original_title: Why Lakers' shooting records in Game 1 could be good news for Rockets
articles.summary: Zach Kram explains that the Los Angeles Lakers’ poor shooting performance in Game 1 could create favorable matchups for the Houston Rockets. The analysis suggests the Rockets can exploit the shooting gap in upcoming games.
key_points: Lakers shot significantly below their season average in Game 1.
The shooting deficit opens strategic opportunities for Houston’s defense.
Kram predicts the Rockets could capitalize on the Lakers’ offensive struggles.

articles.title: Spurs fall to Trail Blazers in Game 2 without Wembanyama
articles.original_title: Takeaways as Spurs lose Game 2 to Trail Blazers without Wemby
articles.summary: The San Antonio Spurs lost Game 2 of the first‑round series to Portland after Victor Wembanyama was unavailable. ESPN’s takeaways highlight how his absence affected the Spurs’ defense and scoring.
key_points: Spurs were unable to replace Wembanyama’s rim protection and playmaking.
Portland exploited the interior void, leading to a decisive win.
The series now hinges on the Spurs’ ability to adjust without their star.

articles.title: Wembanyama suffers concussion in Spurs’ Game 2 loss
articles.original_title: Wemby concussed on hard fall in Spurs' G2 loss
articles.summary: Victor Wembanyama was diagnosed with a concussion after a hard fall in the second quarter of the Spurs’ Game 2 defeat to the Trail Blazers. The injury raises concerns about his availability for the remainder of the series.
key_points: Concussion occurred during a collision in the second quarter.
Spurs lost the game, falling behind in the series.
Wembanyama’s health status will be closely monitored before the next matchup.

articles.title: VJ Edgecombe’s 30‑10 performance lifts 76ers past Celtics
articles.original_title: Edgecombe makes history with 30-10 to stun C's
articles.summary: Playing through a painful back injury, VJ Edgecombe recorded 30 points and 10 rebounds as the Philadelphia 76ers defeated the Boston Celtics 111‑97, tying the series 1‑1. Tyrese Maxey added 29 points in the win.
key_points: Edgecombe’s 30‑10 line came despite an early hard fall on his back.
Philadelphia’s balanced scoring helped even the series against Boston.
Maxey’s 29 points complemented Edgecombe’s effort, highlighting the 76ers’ depth.

articles.title: Scoot Henderson Scores Season‑High 31 to Power Blazers Past Spurs
articles.original_title: Scoot plays his best game of season, fuels Blazers
articles.summary: After a season marred by injuries, Scoot Henderson delivered a breakout performance with 31 points, leading Portland to a victory over San Antonio. The win also evened the first‑round playoff series at 1‑1.
key_points: Henderson posted a career‑best 31 points, his highest total of the 2025‑26 season.
Portland defeated the Spurs, tying the series after losing the opening game.
The performance came as Henderson returned from a stretch of health issues that limited his minutes earlier in the year.

articles.title: Billy Donovan Steps Down as Chicago Bulls Head Coach
articles.original_title: Donovan leaves Bulls amid sweeping changes
articles.summary: Billy Donovan announced his resignation after six seasons at the helm of the Chicago Bulls, citing the organization’s sweeping changes. The departure marks the end of his tenure, during which the Bulls missed the playoffs multiple times.
key_points: Donovan leaves after six years, during which the Bulls failed to secure a postseason berth.
His resignation coincides with a broader restructuring of the Bulls’ front office and coaching staff.
The team will begin a search for a new head coach ahead of the upcoming season.

articles.summary: Addressing speculation about owner Tom Dundon’s reported budget reductions, interim coach Tiago Splitter said his priority remains on‑court performance and player development. He declined to comment on the financial details, emphasizing the need to keep the team competitive.
key_points: Splitter was asked about alleged cost‑cutting measures by new owner Tom Dundon.
He responded that his focus is on basketball and preparing the roster.
No specific information about budget changes was disclosed.

key_points: Duren was limited to four shot attempts in the first game of the series.
He pledged to be more assertive and generate more scoring opportunities in Game 2.
